resources.name: Clatsop Behavioral Healthcare
resources.description: Community mental health center providing outpatient counseling for children, adults, and families; psychiatric medication management; crisis intervention; peer support; case management; substance use disorder treatment; and recovery services.
resources.eligibility: Open to all residents. Insurance requirements vary by program. Some grant-funded services are available regardless of insurance.
resources.sourceNote: Verified via Clatsop Behavioral Healthcare website and 211info on 2026-07-11.

resources.name: Clatsop County 24-Hour Crisis Line
resources.description: Twenty-four-hour crisis line providing immediate support, suicide prevention, crisis counseling, assessment, and referrals for anyone experiencing a mental health crisis.
resources.hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
resources.sourceNote: Verified via Clatsop Behavioral Healthcare and 211info on 2026-07-11.

resources.name: North Coast Respite Center
resources.description: Short-term residential respite program providing a voluntary, recovery-focused alternative to psychiatric hospitalization for adults experiencing behavioral health challenges.
resources.eligibility: Adults meeting admission criteria. Referral or screening required.
resources.sourceNote: Verified via Clatsop Behavioral Healthcare service listings and business directory on 2026-07-11.

resources.name: Clatsop Community Action (CCA)
resources.description: Primary agency for housing, food, and energy assistance in Clatsop County. Operates the regional food bank and provides rental assistance to prevent eviction.

resources.name: CARE Tillamook
resources.description: Provides emergency financial assistance, transitional housing, and family support services including new parent programs for Tillamook County residents.
resources.eligibility: Tillamook County residents. Income restrictions apply for financial aid.

resources.name: Astoria Rescue Mission
resources.description: Faith-based shelter providing emergency beds, daily meals, and clothing for men, women, and families in crisis.

resources.name: Tillamook County Community Health Center
resources.description: Federally Qualified Health Center offering primary medical care, dental, behavioral health, and public health services to all residents.
resources.eligibility: Serves all patients regardless of ability to pay. Sliding scale available.

resources.name: North Coast Food Web
resources.description: Community food center offering cooking classes, local food access programs, and SNAP matching at local farmers markets.

resources.name: Providence Seaside Hospital
resources.description: Full-service hospital providing emergency care, medical imaging, and crisis intervention services on the coast.

resources.name: The Harbor
resources.description: Advocacy, confidential emergency shelter, and support services for survivors of domestic violence, sexual assault, and stalking.

resources.name: Tides of Change
resources.description: Providing hope, safety, and support to survivors of domestic and sexual violence in Tillamook County. 24/7 crisis support and emergency shelter.
resources.eligibility: Survivors of domestic violence and sexual assault.

resources.name: Helping Hands Reentry Outreach
resources.description: Trauma-informed facility offering emergency shelter and long-term reentry programs for individuals experiencing homelessness.
resources.eligibility: Must pass UA and background check for long term.

resources.name: Columbia Pacific Food Bank
resources.description: Regional food bank distributing food to pantries and meal sites across Columbia and Clatsop counties. Direct produce distribution available.
resources.eligibility: Income guidelines apply for USDA food.

resources.name: Food Roots
resources.description: Working to grow a robust and equitable North Coast food system. They offer SNAP match programs at local farmers markets to make fresh produce affordable.

resources.name: Tillamook Family Counseling Center
resources.description: Comprehensive behavioral health services including mental health therapy, substance use disorder treatment, and developmental disability services.
resources.eligibility: Accepts OHP and most private insurance.

resources.name: Oregon Law Center (Serving Coast)
resources.description: Provides free civil legal assistance to low-income individuals. Handles housing, domestic violence, and public benefits cases.

resources.name: 211info
resources.description: Oregon's official resource database. Call, text, or search online for referrals to thousands of health and social services.
